---
title: "Week 12: Final Demo Day & Technical Defense"
subtitle: "Showcasing the Minimum Viable Product (MVP)"
author: "Master's in No-Code Programming"
date: "2026-06-10"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: materia
    code-fold: false
---

# 🎯 Learning Objectives
By the end of this session, students will be able to:
1.  **Pitch** a functional digital solution to a technical and business audience.
2.  **Defend** architectural choices (Stack selection, Data Schema, Logic).
3.  **Handle** real-time technical Q&A regarding scalability and security.
4.  **Demonstrate** a live, bug-free "Happy Path" user journey.

---

# 1. The Event Structure (3 Hours)

The session is divided into 10-minute "Sprints" per student.

## 1.1 The "High-Stakes" Format
* **5 Minutes: The Pitch.** Focusing on the Problem, the Solution, and the Business Value.
* **5 Minutes: The Live Demo.** A walkthrough of the functional app (Airtable/Softr/Make/AI).
* **5 Minutes: Technical Q&A.** The Jury asks deep-dive questions about the "Back-end" logic.



---

# 2. Jury Grading Sheet (Criteria for Success)

Each member of the jury (Faculty + External Guest) uses this rubric:

| Criteria | Weight | Score (0-20) | Notes |
|----------|:------:|:------------:|-------|
| **Problem/Solution Fit** | 20% | | Is the pain point solved effectively? |
| **Technical Complexity** | 30% | | Correct use of Relational DB, APIs, and AI? |
| **UI/UX Quality** | 20% | | Is the interface intuitive and professional? |
| **Architectural Defense**| 20% | | Can the student explain the logic flow? |
| **Presentation Skills** | 10% | | Clarity, timing, and handling of Q&A. |

---

# 3. Student Resource: The Pitch Script Template

To help students prepare, provide this "3-Act" script structure:

### Act 1: The Problem (60 Seconds)
* *"Last month, I realized that [Person X] spends [Time Y] doing [Manual Task Z]. This costs the organization [Amount/Stress]."*

### Act 2: The Solution (120 Seconds - Live Demo)
* *"I built [App Name] using a modular No-Code stack. Here is how a user signs up... Notice how the AI Agent automatically categorizes the input... This is then synced to our Airtable 'Source of Truth' via a Make.com webhook."*

### Act 3: The Technical Defense (60 Seconds)
* *"I chose Xano for the backend to ensure data sovereignty. My logic uses a 'Safety First' filter to prevent unauthorized API access. If we scale to 1,000 users, my current bottleneck would be [X], which I plan to solve by [Y]."*

---

# 4. Closing the Semester: Post-Demo Reflection

::: {.callout-note}
### Final Reflection (Independent Study)
After the demo, students must submit a 250-word reflection on:
*"Which part of the 'Architect' mindset was the hardest to adopt, and how has your view of software changed?"*
:::

---

# 💡 Teacher's Tip
*Demo Day is stressful. Remind students: **'The Jury wants you to succeed.'** If a bug happens during the live demo (and it will), tell them to stay calm, explain what is happening 'under the hood,' and move on. In 2026, the ability to troubleshoot a live system is a more valuable skill than a 'perfect' first run.*

---

**Semester End:** *Grades will be published within 7 days. Congratulations on becoming No-Code Architects!*
